dialogueChunks.intro: Ha! Look at you, walking in like a conquering hero! And rightfully so!
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I knew you had it in you, {player_name}. When I first laid eyes on you, I thought: there's someone who doesn't know the meaning of impossible. The {quest_item} is secured, the route is clear, and {target_npc} is already telling everyone from here to {location} about what we accomplished.
dialogueChunks.outro: Your share of the spoils, as promised. And if you ever want to chase another impossible dream, you know where to find me.

playerResponses.text: Best partnership I've had in years. Until next time.
The treasure was worth the trouble. Barely.
Just hand over my cut and we'll call it even.

dialogueChunks.intro: You're alive. After what happened in there, I honestly didn't expect to see you again.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We got the {quest_item}, yes, but the cost... {target_npc} didn't make it out. The cave collapsed behind you, sealing everything else inside. Half our equipment is buried under rubble. The enterprise succeeded by the narrowest of margins, and I find myself wondering if it was worth the price.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take your share. You earned every piece of it and then some. I need time to think about whether I'll ever attempt something like this again.

playerResponses.text: We knew the risks going in. {target_npc} did too.
Some victories leave scars. This is one of them.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Come here, come here! You won't believe what's happened!
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Word of our enterprise has spread like wildfire! Merchants from three settlements are already bidding on the {quest_item}, and the scholars at {location} want to study it. {target_npc} brokered a deal that will fund a dozen more expeditions. Your name is on everyone's lips: the adventurer who went where no one dared and came back with proof.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here's your share from the initial sale. And mark my words: this is just the beginning. You and I are going to do great things.

playerResponses.text: Fame and fortune in one trip? I could get used to this.
The money is nice, but the adventure was the real reward.
I'll let you handle the fame. Just keep my share coming.
